title: blechnum difforme
intro: Blechnum difforme, also known as the Rough Water Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. It is native to Australia and New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The Rough Water Fern is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion or decoction to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
